---
title: "Systems Programming and Operating-System Words"
---

# Systems Programming and Operating-System Words

Range: `0685-0770`.

## Completion

| Status      | Count |
| ----------- | ----- |
| authored    | 12    |
| stub        | 73    |
| needs_sense | 1     |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                      | Completion  | Summary                                                                           |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------- | ----------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0685  | `STUB` [boot](#rune-0685)                 | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0686  | `STUB` [bootloader](#rune-0686)           | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0687  | `STUB` [capability mode](#rune-0687)      | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0688  | [cgroup](#rune-0688)                      | authored    | A kernel mechanism for bounding resources by group.                               |
| 0689  | `STUB` [chroot](#rune-0689)               | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0690  | `STUB` [context switch](#rune-0690)       | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0691  | `STUB` [cron](#rune-0691)                 | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0692  | `STUB` [daemon {Sys}](#rune-0692)         | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0693  | `STUB` [device](#rune-0693)               | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0694  | `STUB` [device tree](#rune-0694)          | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0695  | `STUB` [direct I/O](#rune-0695)           | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0696  | [driver](#rune-0696)                      | authored    | Software that speaks directly to a device or kernel surface.                      |
| 0697  | [epoll](#rune-0697)                       | authored    | Linux event notification for many file descriptors at once.                       |
| 0698  | `STUB` [exec](#rune-0698)                 | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0699  | `STUB` [exit code](#rune-0699)            | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0700  | [file](#rune-0700)                        | authored    | A named persistent byte stream in a filesystem.                                   |
| 0701  | [file descriptor](#rune-0701)             | authored    | A small integer handle standing in for an open kernel object.                     |
| 0702  | `STUB` [file lock](#rune-0702)            | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0703  | `STUB` [filesystem](#rune-0703)           | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0704  | `STUB` [firmware {Sys}](#rune-0704)       | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0705  | `STUB` [fork {Sys}](#rune-0705)           | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0706  | `STUB` [futex](#rune-0706)                | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0707  | `STUB` [group id](#rune-0707)             | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0708  | `STUB` [init](#rune-0708)                 | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0709  | [inode](#rune-0709)                       | authored    | Filesystem record describing a file apart from its name.                          |
| 0710  | [interrupt](#rune-0710)                   | needs_sense | A priority word from the machine; ordinary flow is preempted for urgent handling. |
| 0711  | `STUB` [ioctl](#rune-0711)                | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0712  | `STUB` [IRQ](#rune-0712)                  | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0713  | `STUB` [isolate {Sys}](#rune-0713)        | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0714  | [kernel](#rune-0714)                      | authored    | The privileged core that arbitrates process, memory, and device access.           |
| 0715  | `STUB` [kernelspace](#rune-0715)          | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0716  | `STUB` [kill signal](#rune-0716)          | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0717  | `STUB` [link layer device](#rune-0717)    | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0718  | `STUB` [load average](#rune-0718)         | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0719  | `STUB` [login shell](#rune-0719)          | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0720  | [mount](#rune-0720)                       | authored    | Attach a filesystem into the visible path tree.                                   |
| 0721  | `STUB` [namespace {Sys}](#rune-0721)      | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0722  | `STUB` [niceness](#rune-0722)             | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0723  | `STUB` [nonblocking I/O](#rune-0723)      | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0724  | `STUB` [open file table](#rune-0724)      | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0725  | `STUB` [page cache](#rune-0725)           | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0726  | `STUB` [page table](#rune-0726)           | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0727  | `STUB` [path](#rune-0727)                 | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0728  | `STUB` [permission {Sys}](#rune-0728)     | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0729  | [pipe](#rune-0729)                        | authored    | A byte stream connecting one process to another.                                  |
| 0730  | `STUB` [poll {Sys}](#rune-0730)           | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0731  | `STUB` [privilege](#rune-0731)            | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0732  | [process {Sys}](#rune-0732)               | authored    | A running program with its own address space and resources.                       |
| 0733  | `STUB` [process table](#rune-0733)        | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0734  | `STUB` [pseudo-terminal](#rune-0734)      | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0735  | `STUB` [pthread](#rune-0735)              | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0736  | `STUB` [quota](#rune-0736)                | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0737  | `STUB` [read-only mount](#rune-0737)      | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0738  | `STUB` [real-time scheduler](#rune-0738)  | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0739  | `STUB` [run queue](#rune-0739)            | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0740  | `STUB` [scheduler class](#rune-0740)      | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0741  | `STUB` [select {Sys}](#rune-0741)         | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0742  | `STUB` [service manager](#rune-0742)      | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0743  | `STUB` [session leader](#rune-0743)       | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0744  | `STUB` [shared memory](#rune-0744)        | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0745  | `STUB` [signal {Sys}](#rune-0745)         | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0746  | `STUB` [socket {Sys}](#rune-0746)         | stub        | A conduit word; a live endpoint for network communication.                        |
| 0747  | `STUB` [softirq](#rune-0747)              | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0748  | `STUB` [spawn {Sys}](#rune-0748)          | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0749  | `STUB` [standard error](#rune-0749)       | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0750  | `STUB` [standard input](#rune-0750)       | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0751  | `STUB` [standard output](#rune-0751)      | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0752  | `STUB` [sticky bit](#rune-0752)           | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0753  | `STUB` [stream descriptor](#rune-0753)    | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0754  | `STUB` [swap](#rune-0754)                 | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0755  | [syscall](#rune-0755)                     | authored    | The controlled crossing from userland into kernel service.                        |
| 0756  | `STUB` [system call boundary](#rune-0756) | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0757  | `STUB` [systemd unit](#rune-0757)         | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0758  | `STUB` [tempfile](#rune-0758)             | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0759  | `STUB` [terminal](#rune-0759)             | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0760  | [thread](#rune-0760)                      | authored    | An independently schedulable line of execution inside a process.                  |
| 0761  | `STUB` [timer](#rune-0761)                | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0762  | `STUB` [trap](#rune-0762)                 | stub        | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.             |
| 0763  | `STUB` [tty](#rune-0763)                  | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0764  | `STUB` [umask](#rune-0764)                | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0765  | `STUB` [unmount](#rune-0765)              | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0766  | `STUB` [user id](#rune-0766)              | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0767  | `STUB` [userland](#rune-0767)             | stub        | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.   |
| 0768  | `STUB` [virtual file system](#rune-0768)  | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |
| 0769  | `STUB` [watchdog](#rune-0769)             | stub        | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.          |
| 0770  | `STUB` [writeback](#rune-0770)            | stub        | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.           |

## Entries

### [0685] boot {#rune-0685}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0686] bootloader {#rune-0686}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0687] capability mode {#rune-0687}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0688] cgroup {#rune-0688}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A kernel mechanism for bounding resources by group.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** A kernel mechanism for bounding resources by group.

### [0689] chroot {#rune-0689}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0690] context switch {#rune-0690}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0691] cron {#rune-0691}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0692] daemon {Sys} {#rune-0692}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0693] device {#rune-0693}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0694] device tree {#rune-0694}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0695] direct I/O {#rune-0695}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0696] driver {#rune-0696}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Software that speaks directly to a device or kernel surface.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** Software that speaks directly to a device or kernel surface.

### [0697] epoll {#rune-0697}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Linux event notification for many file descriptors at once.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** Linux event notification for many file descriptors at once.

### [0698] exec {#rune-0698}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0699] exit code {#rune-0699}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0700] file {#rune-0700}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A named persistent byte stream in a filesystem.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** A named persistent byte stream in a filesystem.

### [0701] file descriptor {#rune-0701}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A small integer handle standing in for an open kernel object.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** A small integer handle standing in for an open kernel object.

### [0702] file lock {#rune-0702}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0703] filesystem {#rune-0703}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0704] firmware {Sys} {#rune-0704}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0705] fork {Sys} {#rune-0705}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0706] futex {#rune-0706}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0707] group id {#rune-0707}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0708] init {#rune-0708}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0709] inode {#rune-0709}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Filesystem record describing a file apart from its name.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** Filesystem record describing a file apart from its name.

### [0710] interrupt {#rune-0710}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A priority word from the machine; ordinary flow is preempted for urgent handling.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0711] ioctl {#rune-0711}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0712] IRQ {#rune-0712}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0713] isolate {Sys} {#rune-0713}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0714] kernel {#rune-0714}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The privileged core that arbitrates process, memory, and device access.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** The privileged core that arbitrates process, memory, and device access.

### [0715] kernelspace {#rune-0715}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0716] kill signal {#rune-0716}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0717] link layer device {#rune-0717}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0718] load average {#rune-0718}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0719] login shell {#rune-0719}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0720] mount {#rune-0720}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Attach a filesystem into the visible path tree.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** Attach a filesystem into the visible path tree.

### [0721] namespace {Sys} {#rune-0721}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0722] niceness {#rune-0722}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0723] nonblocking I/O {#rune-0723}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0724] open file table {#rune-0724}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0725] page cache {#rune-0725}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0726] page table {#rune-0726}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0727] path {#rune-0727}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0728] permission {Sys} {#rune-0728}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0729] pipe {#rune-0729}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A byte stream connecting one process to another.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** A byte stream connecting one process to another.

### [0730] poll {Sys} {#rune-0730}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0731] privilege {#rune-0731}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0732] process {Sys} {#rune-0732}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A running program with its own address space and resources.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** A running program with its own address space and resources.

### [0733] process table {#rune-0733}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0734] pseudo-terminal {#rune-0734}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0735] pthread {#rune-0735}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0736] quota {#rune-0736}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0737] read-only mount {#rune-0737}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0738] real-time scheduler {#rune-0738}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0739] run queue {#rune-0739}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0740] scheduler class {#rune-0740}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0741] select {Sys} {#rune-0741}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0742] service manager {#rune-0742}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0743] session leader {#rune-0743}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0744] shared memory {#rune-0744}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0745] signal {Sys} {#rune-0745}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0746] socket {Sys} {#rune-0746}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A conduit word; a live endpoint for network communication.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0747] softirq {#rune-0747}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0748] spawn {Sys} {#rune-0748}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0749] standard error {#rune-0749}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0750] standard input {#rune-0750}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0751] standard output {#rune-0751}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0752] sticky bit {#rune-0752}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0753] stream descriptor {#rune-0753}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0754] swap {#rune-0754}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0755] syscall {#rune-0755}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The controlled crossing from userland into kernel service.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** The controlled crossing from userland into kernel service.

### [0756] system call boundary {#rune-0756}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0757] systemd unit {#rune-0757}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0758] tempfile {#rune-0758}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0759] terminal {#rune-0759}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0760] thread {#rune-0760}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An independently schedulable line of execution inside a process.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

**Pocket gloss:** An independently schedulable line of execution inside a process.

### [0761] timer {#rune-0761}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0762] trap {#rune-0762}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0763] tty {#rune-0763}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0764] umask {#rune-0764}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0765] unmount {#rune-0765}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0766] user id {#rune-0766}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0767] userland {#rune-0767}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0768] virtual file system {#rune-0768}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0769] watchdog {#rune-0769}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.

### [0770] writeback {#rune-0770}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.

**Shadow:** the host boundary can leak resources, permissions, descriptors, or platform assumptions.
